data.title: U.S. grain futures pressured as export sales fall below forecasts
data.description:
U.S. grain futures fell sharply on Friday after export sales of soybeans and wheat reported by the Department of Agriculture came in below expectations.

In its latest weekly export sales report, the USDA said soybean export sales for the week ended December 26 totaled 484.7K metric tons in the 2024-25 marketing year, down 51% from the previous week and a marketing year low, and wheat sales totaled 140.6K tons in 2024-25, also a marketing year low and down 77% from the previous week.

Analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had forecast soybeans to fall by 500K-1M tons, and wheat to land in the range of 200K-525K tons; corn sales came in at the low end of forecasts, totaling 777K tons for 2024-25.

Wheat for March delivery (W_1:COM) finished -3.2% on Friday to $5.28 1/2 per bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade, March soybeans (S_1:COM) settled -2% to $9.92 per bushel, and March corn (C_1:COM) closed -1.9% to $4.51 1/4 per bushel.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (DBA), (MOO)

Soybeans and soy products have been under pressure on concerns over renewable fuel programs around the world, not just in the U.S., where President-elect Trump is expected to advocate more for fossil fuels.

"Delayed implementation of Indonesia's B40 program is a headwind for vegoils, with palm oil down nearly 2% on the day," John Stewart & Associates wrote, adding that "without 45Z guidelines in the U.S. we may see [soybeans] continue to face headwinds here in the short run."

data.title: Boeing appoints Dana Deasy as chief information officer
data.description:
• Boeing (NYSE:BA) on Friday announced the appointment of Dana Deasy as the company's new chief information digital officer and senior vice president, Information Technology & Data Analytics.
  • Most recently, Deasy served as chief information officer at the U.S. Department of Defense and before that, at JPMorganChase, BP and General Motors.
  • Deasy replaces Susan Doniz, who left the company late last year.
  • BA +0.04% after hours to $169.98.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: cbdMD receives NYSE non-compliance notice
data.description:
• cbdMD (NYSE:YCBD) on Friday said it has received a NYSE notice, notifying the company that it is no longer in compliance with the continued listing standard as it reported stockholders' equity of $1,963,417 as of September 30, 2024 and losses from continuing operations and/or net losses in four of its five most recent fiscal years then ended.
  • The company said it is committed to undertaking a transaction or transactions in the future to achieve compliance with the NYSE American's requirements, including but not limited to seeking shareholder approval to convert the preferred stock and its accrued dividend, a liability totaling $4.67 million on September 30, 2024, into shares of common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook plans to donate $1M to Trump inauguration: report
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) CEO Tim Cook plans to donate $1M to President-elect Donald Trump's inaugural committee, according to a report by Axios.
  • Cook also attended a dinner with Trump at Mar-a-Lago last month, as well as established a relationship with Trump during his first term as president. The Alabama native believes the presidential inauguration is an important U.S. tradition, and donating to it symbolizes unity, sources told Axios. However, Apple is not expected to contribute.
  • The move by Cook also reflects a larger warming of the relationship between Silicon Valley and the incoming administration. Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META) are each donating $1M to the inauguration. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman also pledged $1M to the inauguration fund.
  • This represents a shift from the 2017 inauguration, when Amazon donated $58,000 and Meta reportedly donated nothing to the event.
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: 'Limited avenues moving forward' for US Steel, Nippon Steel after Biden scuttles deal
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) shares settled -6.5% while ADRs of Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) ended -0.8% on Friday after President Biden officially blocked the proposed merger of the two steel producers, likely dealing a fatal blow to the contentious plan after a year of review.

The companies blasted Biden's decision, calling it a "clear violation of due process" that failed to present any "credible evidence of a national security issue," and ripped the U.S. government's review process, saying that "instead of abiding by the law, the process was manipulated to advance President Biden's political agenda."

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF), which faces a $565M penalty payment to US Steel (X) following the deal's collapse, said it would "take all appropriate action" to protect its legal rights, but any legal challenge against the U.S. government would be difficult, since U.S. statute makes it clear that American presidents are granted the power to kill a deal they deem a threat to national security.

The companies might try to make an argument on procedural grounds; the government typically gives companies a warning that a deal has problems that need mitigation, and then are given time to address those issues, but the steel firms were not offered the same warning time, which could leave the door open for litigation - but still an uphill climb.

It seems questionable that another buyer will emerge: US Steel (X) has reported nine consecutive quarters of declining profits amid a global downturn in the steel industry, and Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF), which previously bid for the company, has seen its share price fall so much that its market value has dropped below US Steel.

J.P. Morgan's Bill Peterson sees "limited avenues moving forward" for a deal, as foreign ownership for US Steel (X) is "seemingly off the table."

"Despite Japan being both a close ally and the largest foreign investor in the U.S., we view the deal's demise as a clear deterrent for foreign entrants interested in buying entry into the U.S. steel market," Peterson wrote.

data.title: Creative Realities announces retirement of CFO Will Logan
data.description:
• Creative Realities (NASDAQ:CREX) on Friday said that Will Logan has resigned as chief financial officer, effective January 31st, 2025.
  • He will serve as a strategic advisor to the Company minimally through the end of 2025.
  • The company said it is evaluating internal and external candidates to succeed Logan.
  • Ryan Mudd, the company’s current controller, will serve as interim chief financial officer effective upon the departure of Logan, stated the company. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MicroStrategy to target a capital raise of up to $2B of preferred stock
data.description:
• MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) said on Friday that it is targeting a capital raise of up to $2 billion through one or more public underwritten offerings of perpetual preferred stock, which will be senior to its class A common stock.
  • The perpetual preferred stock may include features such as (i) convertibility to its class A common stock, (ii) payment of cash dividends, and (iii) provisions allowing for redemptions of shares, among other features.
  • The number of depositary shares representing an interest in the perpetual preferred stock to be offered, the final terms of the perpetual preferred stock, and the price for the offering have not yet been determined.
  • The purpose of the offering is to allow MicroStrategy to continue to strengthen its balance sheet and acquire more bitcoin.
  • The offering is expected to occur, if at all, in the first quarter of 2025.
  • MicroStrategy (MSTR) shares were up 0.19% in post-market trading.

data.title: Forge Global receives continued listing standards notice from the NYSE
data.description:
• Forge Global Holdings (NYSE:FRGE) said that it is not in compliance with Rule 802.01C of the NYSE’s Listed Company Manual.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing of the company’s common stock on the NYSE during the six-month cure period.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bianco: Largest yield rise after rate cuts in 4 decades is a ‘rejection’ of Fed policy
data.description:
The jump in the 10-year yield (10USY) while the Federal Reserve slashes interest rates signals bond investors take issue with the easing policy, according to Bianco Research founder Jim Bianco.

“It is a rejection of the cutting policy and a material change in financial conditions,” Bianco said in a Thursday post on X (formerly Twitter) that focused on the benchmark yield (US10Y). The yield recently pushed above 4.6%, the highest since late May.

Bianco highlighted that the yield (US10Y) had risen by 91 basis points over a stretch of 107 days since the Fed started rate-cutting on September 18.

“Only 1981 saw a larger increase in rates,” he said. Yields rise as bond prices fall. His chart below shows the cumulative change in 10-year yields (US10Y) in the first 115 days after the Fed has launched easing campaigns, running back to 1963:

The Fed started with a large half-point cut and through December, drew the fed funds rate down by 100 basis points, to 4.25%-4.5%.

“Inflation data is looking a little sticky over the last several months, and [Fed Chair Jerome Powell] cut rates based on this idea that there was some weakness in the labor market, and it's not there,” Bianco said in an interview posted Thursday on Bianco Research’s YouTube channel.

The Federal Open Market Committee last month raised its core PCE projections for 2025 and 2026, and sees the core inflation rate hitting its 2% target in 2027.

The “market is telling [Powell] with this 100-basis-point rise in the 10-year yield (US10Y) since September, ‘We don't need these cuts,’" Bianco said in the Real Vision interview.

The Fed was monitoring rising bond yields to “see where they settle,” Powell said at a November press conference.

“[It’s] not really our job to provide our specific decomposition. I will say, though, that it appears that the moves are not, not principally about higher inflation expectations,” Powell said. “They’re really about a sense of more likely to have stronger growth and perhaps less in the wayof downside risks.”

On Friday, the 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.60%.

Investors can track Treasury-market moves through ETFs including: (IEF), (TLT) and (SCHO).

data.title: Tandy Leather Factory appoints Johan Hedberg as CEO
data.description:
• Tandy Leather Factory (NASDAQ:TLF) on Friday said it has appointed Johan Hedberg as its new chief executive officer and a member of the board of directors, effective as of Monday, January 6.
  • Hedberg most recently served as chief sales officer and president, Americas, of Fiskars Group.
  • The company elected John Sullivan to join its board of directors, also as of January 6.
  • TLF closed -0.41% at $4.75.
  • Source: Press Release
